titleEn: Tested: first Einböck intra-row precision hoe
titleZh: 首台Einböck行内精准中耕机实地测试
summaryZh: 奥地利中耕专家Einböck推出新型Invera行内精准中耕机，采用捷克Ullmanna植物识别技术，在荷兰甜菜田完成首次作业。尽管潮湿天气给摄像头深度调节带来挑战，但机器仍以约2公里/小时的速度精准除草。该技术结合Claas Culticam相机，展示了智能视觉在田间杂草管理中的最新应用，对智慧种植领域具有参考价值。
translationZh:
在充满挑战的潮湿条件下，4行Einböck Invera于6月9日在Eexterveen（德伦特省）的甜菜田完成了首次作业。部分由于潮湿条件，摄像头最初在正确控制每个元件的深度调节方面遇到了一些困难。当进口商Reesink Agri的Christiaan Borkus在平板上进行最终调整时，刀片仍以约2公里/小时的速度整齐地绕甜菜植株除草。

在当前市场（委婉地说）处于低谷之际，奥地利中耕专家Einböck推出了其新型Invera行内精准中耕机。其最强大的资产或许是来自捷克公司Ullmanna的植物识别技术。这使得Invera更具特色。对于Einböck，荷兰进口商Reesink Agri在中耕技术方面一直握有一张好牌。这些机器以坚固耐用著称。结合Claas Culticam相机和
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了视觉识别与精准机械除草在田间的最新结合，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解跨领域自动化技术趋势有启发意义。
contentFull: Tested: first Einböck intra-row precision hoe 23-06 | Crop solutions | ArticleUnder challenging, wet conditions, the 4-row Einböck Invera made its first passes in a sugar beet field in Eexterveen (Drenthe) on 9 June. Partly because of the wet conditions, the cameras initially had some difficulty controlling the depth adjustment per element properly. While Christiaan Borkus of importer Reesink Agri made the final adjustments on the tablet, the blades nevertheless hoed neatly around the beet plants at about 2 km/h. Photos: Hans BanusIn a market that is currently, to put it mildly, on its knees, Austrian hoeing specialist Einböck is presenting its new Invera intra-row precision hoe. Perhaps its strongest asset is plant recognition from the Czech company Ullmanna. This makes the Invera even more distinctive. With Einböck, Dutch importer Reesink Agri has always had a strong card to play when it comes to hoeing technology. The machines are known for being solid and robust.
